operating_state: Total net sales increased 5% to $95.4 billion in Q2 2025, driven by iPhone Pro models, Mac products, iPad Air, and Services growth from advertising, App Store, and cloud services. Regional performance showed broad strength with double-digit growth in Japan and solid gains in Americas and Rest of Asia Pacific, while Europe remained flat and Greater China declined modestly.
cost_structure: Total gross margin percentage improved to 47.1% from 46.6% year-over-year, with Services margin expanding to 75.7% due to favorable service mix, while Products margin compressed to 35.9% due to unfavorable product mix and foreign currency headwinds. Operating expenses increased driven by higher research and development headcount, infrastructure costs, and variable selling expenses.
material_events: Apple launched iPhone 16e, iPad Air, iPad, MacBook Air, and Mac Studio during Q2 2025. The company repurchased $25.0 billion of common stock and paid $3.8 billion in dividends. Apple released €14.2 billion from escrow to Ireland, fully settling the State Aid Decision obligation. The Board authorized an additional $100 billion share repurchase program and raised the quarterly dividend from $0.25 to $0.26 per share effective Q3 2025.
macro_exposures: New U.S. tariffs were announced targeting imports from China, India, Japan, South Korea, Taiwan, Vietnam, and the EU, with reciprocal tariffs and retaliatory measures imposed by several countries in response. The U.S. Department of Commerce initiated a Section 232 investigation into semiconductor imports. Foreign currency weakness relative to the U.S. dollar created unfavorable impacts across all regions, with particular pressure from renminbi and yen weakness.
forward_outlook: Management maintains confidence in liquidity through cash balances, operational cash generation, and debt market access. Gross margins are expected to remain subject to volatility from pricing pressures, competition, component costs, and product mix shifts. The company intends to increase its dividend annually and continues capital return programs with $40.8 billion remaining under current authorization plus the new $100 billion program.
uncertainty_profile: Manufacturing purchase obligations totaled $38.4 billion payable within 12 months as of March 29, 2025. Pending accounting standard implementations include enhanced disclosure requirements with effective dates ranging from Q4 2025 to Q4 2028. Trade measure impacts remain uncertain depending on additional tariff announcements and retaliatory responses.
